petitioner_background: The petitioner filed an Immigrant Petition for Alien Worker seeking classification as an alien of extraordinary ability under Section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act. No substantive information about the petitioner's field, nationality, or qualifications is provided in the decision, as the matter was resolved on procedural grounds without reaching the merits of the extraordinary ability claim.
outcome_reasoning: The appeal was rejected as untimely filed and the matter was returned to the director for consideration as a motion to reopen. The director issued the decision on March 30, 2009, giving the petitioner 33 days to file the appeal. Although the petitioner submitted Form I-290B on April 29, 2009, it was not properly signed at Part 4 and was rejected as improperly filed. The petitioner resubmitted a properly signed Form I-290B on May 5, 2009, which was 36 days after the decision was issued, making the appeal untimely. Because the untimely appeal meets the requirements of a motion to reopen, the AAO returned the matter to the service center director to consider it as a motion to reopen and render a new decision.
legal_citations: Section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act
fraud_or_procedural_issues: The appeal was rejected as untimely filed due to a procedural defect. The petitioner initially submitted Form I-290B on April 29, 2009, but Part 4 of the form was not signed, causing it to be rejected as improperly filed under 8 C.F.R. § 103.2(a)(7)(i) (rejected petitions do not retain a filing date). The director issued a notice on April 30, 2009 requesting a signed resubmission, and the properly signed form was filed on May 5, 2009—36 days after the March 30, 2009 decision, exceeding the 33-day deadline. The director erroneously annotated the appeal as timely and forwarded it to the AAO.
